About

The homepage introduces Darrell as a consultant and advisor first, then uses the blog to signal current thinking areas. It is designed to feel credible for customer conversations, recruiting visibility, and thought leadership.

Positioning statement

I help organizations modernize end-user computing strategies, evaluate workspace platforms, and deliver pragmatic architecture decisions. This site is where I’ll share field lessons, implementation patterns, and design insights from consulting engagements.

What visitors should understand quickly

  • Consulting depth
    Built from a career spanning engineering, architecture, and operations roles.
  • Modern focus
    Structured for Azure, endpoint, and EUC-oriented thought leadership.
  • Publishing readiness
    A blog-first layout with expandable topic lanes instead of fake sample posts.

Career path

The experience section keeps the homepage anchored in real credibility while staying selective enough to support a clean publishing brand.

1
AHEAD · Senior Technical Consultant April 2023 to present · Chicago

Current anchor role for the site’s authority and point of view.

2
Mainline Information Systems · EUC Engineer January 2021 to April 2023

Strengthens the end-user computing and delivery narrative.

3
Balyasny Asset Management · Engineer, Technical Architecture June 2020 to January 2021

Adds architecture depth and enterprise decision-making credibility.

4
CANCOM · Platform Architect January 2017 to June 2020

Supports platform strategy, modernization, and technical leadership positioning.

5
Earlier consulting and engineering roles EMC, Nuveen, Esurance, Discover, Zurich, and more

Signals broad industry exposure without overcrowding the homepage.

Expertise and proof points

These cards help visitors understand what kind of writing and consulting perspective they can expect from the blog.

Consulting themes

  • Workspace platform strategy
    Comparing, assessing, and modernizing digital workspace environments.
  • Endpoint and policy design
    Practical guidance for modern management, configuration, and user experience.
  • Technical communication
    Clear diagrams, explainers, and executive-ready architecture storytelling.

  • Vendor Management
    Useful for advisory posts and customer-facing decision frameworks.
  • Visio
    Supports diagram-led content and architecture communication.

Certifications

How this supports the blog

Visitors get an immediate sense that the articles come from hands-on delivery experience, not generic commentary. That matters when the audience includes technical leaders, customers, and peers looking for grounded guidance.

Blog launch themes

Instead of inventing articles, the site ships with interactive topic lanes so the homepage stays honest until real posts are published.

Publishing lane · Azure Virtual Desktop

Ideas ready for future posts

  • What good AVD assessment scopes should include before design begins.
  • Common rollout pitfalls in pilot-to-production transitions.
  • How to explain tradeoffs to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Launch posture

The site is deployable now, even before the first article is published.

This keeps the brand experience clean while giving you an easy path to add real posts later.

Publishing lane · Microsoft Intune

Ideas ready for future posts

  • Migration lessons from legacy device management to modern policy design.
  • How to communicate configuration decisions to customer teams.
  • Where operational simplicity matters more than feature sprawl.
Audience fit

This lane works well for customer-facing explainers and architecture notes.

It also supports future posts that can be repurposed into slide content or assessments.

Publishing lane · Citrix modernization

Ideas ready for future posts

  • When a Citrix assessment should lead to optimization versus platform change.
  • Frameworks for comparing operational complexity across options.
  • Modernization narratives that resonate with decision-makers.
Consulting value

This positions the blog as practical and advisory rather than product-centric.

It creates strong continuity with assessment and strategy work.

Publishing lane · Architecture communication

Ideas ready for future posts

  • How diagrams change executive conversations when they are designed for the audience.
  • Ways to make technical assessments easier to consume and act on.
  • Turning delivery experience into reusable client-ready narratives.
Brand advantage

This lane differentiates the site from generic consultant portfolios by emphasizing clarity and communication.

It helps your future content feel useful on day one.

Suggested launch checklist

  • Replace the placeholder positioning statement with your preferred voice.
  • Publish your first real article in one of the four topic lanes.
  • Deploy the package to Azure App Service using the Node runtime.
Built as a lightweight single-page blog website for Azure App Service deployment.
